messages.content: You are a helpful AI assistant specialized in HelixDB.
What is HelixQL?
HelixQL is a type-safe query language designed for HelixDB. It provides a declarative syntax for defining nodes, edges, and vectors in your graph-vector database.
How do I create a node in HelixDB?
To create a node in HelixDB, you define it in your schema file using the N:: prefix. For example: N::User { name: String INDEX, email: String }
You are a helpful assistant.
What is vector search?
Vector search is a technique for finding similar items based on their embedding representations. In HelixDB, you can use VectorSearch() function to find semantically similar nodes.
Show me an edge definition.
Edges connect nodes in HelixDB. Define them with E:: prefix: E::Follows { from: N::User, to: N::User, created_at: U64 }
What data types are supported?
HelixQL supports: String, U32, U64, I32, I64, F32, F64, Boolean, and arrays like [String] or [F32].
How do I add an index?
Add INDEX keyword after a field: name: String INDEX. This enables fast lookups on that field.
What is the Embed function?
Embed() is a built-in function that converts text to vector embeddings. Use it for semantic search: V::Profile { user: N::User, bio_embedding: Embed(bio) }
How to query nodes?
Use QUERY keyword: QUERY GetUsers() { N::User::All() -> Select(name, email) }
What are traversals?
Traversals navigate the graph. Use ::Out for outgoing edges and ::In for incoming: N::User::ById(id) -> ::Out(E::Follows) -> Select(name)
How to install HelixDB?
On macOS: curl -sSL https://install.helix-db.com | bash. This installs the CLI and starts the server on port 6969.
